Creamy Lemon Pepper Parmesan Chicken

4.5
(4)
Add your rating & review
3 Photos

This creamy lemon pepper Parmesan chicken features tender chicken thighs simmered in an easy sauce with lemon zest, black pepper, and Parmesan cheese. It's quick enough for any weeknight dinner, and would be nice over rice or orzo.

By
Diana Moutsopoulos
Diana Moutsopoulos
Diana Moutsopoulos is a food writer, recipe developer, Greek cuisine expert, and Senior Editorial Director at Allrecipes.
Allrecipes' editorial guidelines
Published on April 12, 2026
Tested by
Barrett Heald
Tested by Barrett Heald
Barrett Heald is a recipe tester and recipe developer in the People Inc. Test Kitchen helping to create and perfect recipes for brands, such as AllRecipes, Better Homes & Gardens, and Midwest Living
Meet the Allrecipes Test Kitchen
3
3
Prep Time:
5 mins
Cook Time:
20 mins
Total Time:
25 mins
Servings:
4
Keep Screen Awake

Ingredients

Oops! Something went wrong. Our team is working on it.

This recipe was developed at its original yield. Ingredient amounts are automatically adjusted, but cooking times and steps remain unchanged. Note that not all recipes scale perfectly. Original recipe (1X) yields 4 servings

  • 8 skinless, boneless chicken thighs

  • 1 1/2 teaspoons freshly ground black pepper, divided

  • 3/4 teaspoon salt, divided

  • 2 tablespoons olive oil

  • 1 medium shallot, chopped

  • 3 garlic cloves, minced

  • 1 cup chicken broth

  • 1 cup heavy cream

  • 1/2 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese

  • 1 teaspoon lemon zest

  • chopped fresh parsley, for garnish

  • lemon wedges, for serving

Directions

Ingredients

Keep Screen Awake
Oops! Something went wrong. Our team is working on it.

This recipe was developed at its original yield. Ingredient amounts are automatically adjusted, but cooking times and steps remain unchanged. Note that not all recipes scale perfectly. Original recipe (1X) yields 4 servings

  • 8 skinless, boneless chicken thighs

  • 1 1/2 teaspoons freshly ground black pepper, divided

  • 3/4 teaspoon salt, divided

  • 2 tablespoons olive oil

  • 1 medium shallot, chopped

  • 3 garlic cloves, minced

  • 1 cup chicken broth

  • 1 cup heavy cream

  • 1/2 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese

  • 1 teaspoon lemon zest

  • chopped fresh parsley, for garnish

  • lemon wedges, for serving

  1. Season chicken thighs evenly on both sides with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper.

  2. Heat olive oil in a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken thighs and cook until browned, turning once, 7 to 8 minutes total. Remove chicken from skillet and set aside.

  3. Add shallot and garlic to the same skillet. Cook, stirring, until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes. Pour in chicken broth and heavy cream. Bring to a gentle simmer.

  4. Stir in Parmesan cheese, lemon zest, remaining 1 teaspoon black pepper, and remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt. Simmer until the sauce thickens slightly, 4 to 5 minutes.

  5. Return chicken to the skillet and cook until heated through, a thermometer inserted into the thickest portion registers 165 degrees F (74 degrees C), and chicken is coated with sauce, 2 to 3 minutes.

  6. Garnish with parsley and serve with lemon wedges.

Recipe developed by Jaelyn Luong

Did you make this recipe?
Something went wrong. Please reload and try again.

985 Calories
67g Fat
31g Carbs
76g Protein

Photos of Creamy Lemon Pepper Parmesan Chicken

01 of 03
Photo by 

Photographer: Jason Donnelly
02 of 03
Photo by  nicolekrystyn
04/16/2026
OK with tweaks Might try again
The cream, pepper, and parmesan really came through in this dish but the lemon flavor was lacking for me. Next time I would add in more lemon zest. I followed the timing of the recipe but my chicken thighs must've be thick and needed almost an additional 10 minutes in the cream sauce to get to the proper internal temperature. This extra time allowed the sauce to reduce even more making it coat the chicken nicely.
03 of 03
Photo by  1010lisalynn
04/16/2026
Creamy Lemon Pepper Parmesan ChickenLovely Parmesan Flavor!I’m obsessed with all the combination of ingredients. The mix of French and Italian cuisines make this dish classic. Shallots with Parmesan and heavy cream can do no wrong in cooking.I found the sauce to be a bit too thin as I don’t like food that splashes at you. Perhaps a bit less chicken broth could help. I added a little extra Parmesan and eventually a little shredded jack cheese – still a bit running and I don’t recommend the Jack cheese. I like chicken thighs but next time I will try with chicken breast or even chicken breast tenders. The only other change I made was too add garlic paste rather than mince garlic. My chicken thighs were thick, and additional cooking time was required.
  1. 1
